title: Episteme ledger entry
description: One curated-memory entry. ledger.jsonl holds one of these objects per line (NDJSON). Source of truth shared by the v1 Python validator and the phase-2 Pi (TS) extension.

id.description: Stable id, e.g. led-0007. Append-only; never reused.

type.description: decision = a choice made; constraint = a rule that must hold; finding = an observed fact about the codebase; assumption = an unverified inference; contract_version = a snapshot pointer when contract.md changed.

source.description: Where it came from, e.g. 'contract AC-4', 'grep src/users', 'verdict turn 7', 'story-12 brainstorm'.

authority.description: verified = an oracle or direct observation backs it; assumed = inferred, not yet verified. A hypothesis stays 'assumed' until an oracle flips it to 'verified'.

oracle_ref.description: For verified entries: the command/test that backs it, e.g. 'npm test -- auth.spec.ts::rejects-expired-token'.

created_at_turn.description: Loop turn index when this entry was written (freshness).

supersedes.description: Id of an earlier entry this one replaces (amend-not-overwrite). Null if none.

feature.description: Which contract/feature this entry belongs to, e.g. 'contract-auth'. Null for project-wide entries.

then.description: A 'verified' entry must cite either an oracle_ref or at least one evidence_for item. No verification without evidence.
